indexName: Baroda BNP Paribas NIFTY Bank ETF

holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Largest Nifty Bank constituent. Post-merger with HDFC Ltd, now India's largest home loan lender and most profitable private bank. NIM ~4%, GNPA <1.5%. 8,000+ branches, 25M+ customers. Retail, SME and wholesale banking in balance.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: 2nd largest. ROE 18%+, NIM 4.3%. Best-in-class digital app (iMobile, 40M+ users). High-quality retail portfolio; corporate and SME loan book growing at 20%+. International business (UK, US, Singapore) adds diversification.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: Ultra-conservative private bank. Low NPA (<1% GNPA), high CET1 ratio (18%+), no large corporate stress. Kotak 811 digital bank has 50M+ accounts. Kotak Securities, AMC and Life Insurance subsidiaries add wealth management value.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: 3rd largest private bank by assets. ROE recovering to 15-16% post-Citibank consumer business acquisition. Axis Pay UPI and Magnus card lead premium segment. Rural lending expanding via GPS mapping and fintech partnerships.

holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.description: India's largest bank by assets with 22,000+ branches. YONO super app has 70M+ users. NPA cleanup complete; return ratios recovering. Key beneficiary of PSU credit upcycle. SBI Cards and SBI Life are highvalue subsidiaries.

holdings.name: IndusInd Bank
holdings.description: Focused on commercial vehicles (25% of book), diamond merchants and consumer finance. Microfinance arm Bharat Financial (BFIL) provides rural reach. Derivative income and non-credit revenue contribute 30%+ of total income.
